test.details: 1032 tests in 122 suites passed after 42.866 seconds

typecheck.command: xcodebuild -scheme NetMonitor-iOS -configuration Debug build && xcodebuild -scheme NetMonitor-macOS -configuration Debug build
typecheck.details: Both iOS and macOS targets built successfully with strict concurrency

lint.details: SwiftLint: warnings only (no errors). SwiftFormat: 0/505 files require formatting

highlights: Misleading 'efficient incremental' comment on O(n) full rebuild in updateDownsampled
Undocumented deviation from startContinuousMeasurement() spec (justified but not commented)
Hardcoded 25m² room size for coverage percentage
Missing nonisolated rationale comment on position provider
Unused Metal GPU objects (MTLDevice, MTLTexture×2, MTLCommandQueue) wasting ~32MB VRAM
Straight-vs-premultiplied alpha blending inconsistency at heatmap splat edges
Pinch-to-zoom/pan gestures don't accumulate across gesture sessions (jumpy behavior)
Heavy main-thread compositing loop (4M pixels at 10Hz)
Potential range crash in rasterizeFloorArea with inverted coordinates
Minor test count discrepancy in handoff (claims 22, actual 18)
PostScanReviewView zoom/pan gestures don't accumulate state between sequences
BSSIDTransition stores raw MAC addresses without redaction
ScanThermalManager tests test a helper function, not the actual manager
Redundant progress state updates in finishScan

appliedUpdates.description: Canonicalized AR service path to Services/AR/ (removed 'or Platform/AR/' ambiguity)
Documented Phase 3 CPU-based rendering architecture decision (HeatmapMetalRenderer uses CPU pixel arrays despite name)
Documented Phase 3 thermal management policy (nominal→continue, serious→reduceMesh, critical→autoPause)

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add SwiftUI gesture accumulation convention: For map/canvas interactions with pinch-to-zoom and drag gestures, use @GestureState for in-progress gesture values and accumulate into @State on .onEnded. Direct assignment of gesture values to @State causes zoom/pan to reset between gesture sequences.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both ContinuousScanView.swift and PostScanReviewView.swift have gesture handlers that directly assign MagnificationGesture/DragGesture values without accumulating across gesture sessions. Two independent workers made the same mistake, indicating this is a systemic pattern gap.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Extend the BSSID/MAC data handling convention beyond just logging. Current convention says 'Never log raw IPs, MACs, SSIDs, or hostnames outside LogSanitizer wrappers' but doesn't address in-memory storage in structs passed to views. Consider: MACs in model types are acceptable, but should be redacted before display in UI text.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: BSSIDTransition in ContinuousScanViewModel.swift stores raw MAC addresses. PostScanReviewView receives them for roaming overlay display. The logging convention covers os.Logger but not UI display.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add to ios-ui-worker skill: (1) Document rsync fallback for mac-mini file sync when git HTTPS auth is broken: 'rsync -avz --exclude .git . mac-mini:~/Projects/NetMonitor-2.0/'. (2) Clarify that adding new .swift files requires xcodegen regeneration even when project.yml itself is unchanged.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All three Phase 3 workers encountered the mac-mini git auth issue and used rsync workaround. Worker for phase3-pause-resume-finish noted that new files require xcodegen regeneration which the skill doesn't mention.

rejectedObservations.observation: Missing pipeline debug logging in ContinuousCapturePipeline.swift (imports os but has zero Logger calls)
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a developer preference, not a convention violation. The ViewModel logs key lifecycle events. Adding debug logging to the pipeline actor is an enhancement, not a correction.

rejectedObservations.observation: ios-ui-worker skill file not found at .factory/skills/ios-ui-worker/SKILL.md
rejectedObservations.reason: Skills may be stored in different locations (personal ~/.factory/droids/ vs project .factory/droids/). The skill exists and workers reference it successfully. The reviewer's file path search was too narrow.

rejectedObservations.observation: rsync workaround not in AGENTS.md Known Pre-Existing Issues (from metal-rendering reviewer)
rejectedObservations.reason: Duplicate of the skill update suggestion. The rsync workaround belongs in the worker skill, not in AGENTS.md which documents issues not workarounds.
